@@ -0,0 +1,166 @@
+"""Tests for concurrent async file operations."""
+
+import asyncio
+import os
+import pytest
+import aiofiles
+
+
+class TestConcurrentRead:
+    """Test concurrent file reading operations."""
+
+    async def test_concurrent_read_multiple_files(self, tmp_path):
+        """Test concurrently reading multiple files."""
+        files = {}
+        for i in range(5):
+            fpath = str(tmp_path / f"file{i}.txt")
+            with open(fpath, "w") as f:
+                f.write(f"Content {i}")
+            files[i] = fpath
+
+        async def read_file(i):
+            async with aiofiles.open(files[i], "r") as f:
+                return await f.read()
+
+        tasks = [asyncio.create_task(read_file(i)) for i in range(5)]
+        results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks)
+        for i, result in enumerate(results):
+            assert result == f"Content {i}"
+
+    async def test_concurrent_read_same_file(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test concurrently reading the same file multiple times."""
+        async def read():
+            async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+                return await f.read()
+
+        results = await asyncio.gather(read(), read(), read())
+        for result in results:
+            assert result == "Line 1\nLine 2\nLine 3\n"
+
+
+class TestConcurrentWrite:
+    """Test concurrent file writing operations."""
+
+    async def test_concurrent_write_multiple_files(self, tmp_path):
+        """Test concurrently writing to multiple files."""
+        async def write_file(i, content):
+            fpath = str(tmp_path / f"out{i}.txt")
+            async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "w") as f:
+                await f.write(content)
+
+        tasks = [
+            asyncio.create_task(write_file(i, f"data_{i}"))
+            for i in range(10)
+        ]
+        await asyncio.gather(*tasks)
+
+        for i in range(10):
+            fpath = str(tmp_path / f"out{i}.txt")
+            with open(fpath, "r") as f:
+                assert f.read() == f"data_{i}"
+
+    async def test_concurrent_read_write(self, tmp_path):
+        """Test concurrent read and write operations."""
+        fpath = str(tmp_path / "rw_test.txt")
+        async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "w") as f:
+            await f.write("Initial content\n")
+
+        async def read_all():
+            async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "r") as f:
+                return await f.read()
+
+        async def append_line():
+            async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "a") as f:
+                await f.write("Appended\n")
+
+        # Write then read concurrently
+        tasks = [
+            asyncio.create_task(append_line()),
+            asyncio.create_task(append_line()),
+        ]
+        await asyncio.gather(*tasks)
+
+        result = await read_all()
+        assert "Initial content" in result
+        assert result.count("Appended") == 2
+
+
+class TestConcurrentMixed:
+    """Test mixed concurrent operations."""
+
+    async def test_concurrent_text_and_binary(self, tmp_path):
+        """Test concurrent text and binary file operations."""
+        text_path = str(tmp_path / "mixed.txt")
+        bin_path = str(tmp_path / "mixed.bin")
+
+        async def write_text():
+            async with aiofiles.open(text_path, "w") as f:
+                await f.write("text data")
+
+        async def write_binary():
+            async with aiofiles.open(bin_path, "wb") as f:
+                await f.write(b"binary data")
+
+        await asyncio.gather(write_text(), write_binary())
+
+        with open(text_path, "r") as f:
+            assert f.read() == "text data"
+        with open(bin_path, "rb") as f:
+            assert f.read() == b"binary data"
+
+    async def test_gather_with_errors(self, tmp_path):
+        """Test error handling in concurrent operations."""
+        fpath = str(tmp_path / "error_test.txt")
+        async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "w") as f:
+            await f.write("ok")
+
+        async def good_read():
+            async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "r") as f:
+                return await f.read()
+
+        async def bad_read():
+            async with aiofiles.open("/nonexistent", "r") as f:
+                return await f.read()
+
+        results = await asyncio.gather(
+            good_read(),
+            bad_read(),
+            return_exceptions=True,
+        )
+        assert results[0] == "ok"
+        assert isinstance(results[1], FileNotFoundError)
+
+    async def test_semaphore_controlled(self, tmp_path):
+        """Test using semaphore to control concurrency."""
+        async def bounded_write(i, sem):
+            async with sem:
+                fpath = str(tmp_path / f"sem{i}.txt")
+                async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "w") as f:
+                    await f.write(f"bounded {i}")
+
+        sem = asyncio.Semaphore(2)
+        tasks = [asyncio.create_task(bounded_write(i, sem)) for i in range(10)]
+        await asyncio.gather(*tasks)
+
+        for i in range(10):
+            fpath = str(tmp_path / f"sem{i}.txt")
+            with open(fpath, "r") as f:
+                assert f.read() == f"bounded {i}"
+
+    async def test_task_group(self, tmp_path):
+        """Test with asyncio.TaskGroup (if available)."""
+        fpath = str(tmp_path / "tg_test.txt")
+        async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "w") as f:
+            await f.write("TaskGroup test\n")
+
+        results = []
+
+        async def read_and_store():
+            async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "r") as f:
+                content = await f.read()
+                results.append(content)
+
+        # Use gather since TaskGroup may not be available in all Python 3.9+
+        await asyncio.gather(read_and_store(), read_and_store())
+        assert len(results) == 2
+        assert all("TaskGroup test" in r for r in results)

+"""Tests for async file opening (aiofiles.open)."""
+
+import asyncio
+import pytest
+import aiofiles
+
+
+class TestAiofilesOpen:
+    """Test the aiofiles.open() function."""
+
+    async def test_open_text_read(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test opening and reading a text file."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+            content = await f.read()
+        assert content == "Line 1\nLine 2\nLine 3\n"
+
+    async def test_open_text_write(self, tmp_path):
+        """Test opening and writing to a text file."""
+        fpath = str(tmp_path / "write_test.txt")
+        async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "w") as f:
+            n = await f.write("Hello aiofiles\n")
+        assert n == 15
+        with open(fpath, "r") as f:
+            assert f.read() == "Hello aiofiles\n"
+
+    async def test_open_binary_read(self, sample_binary_file):
+        """Test opening and reading a binary file."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_binary_file, "rb") as f:
+            data = await f.read()
+        assert data == b"\x00\x01\x02\x03\x04\x05"
+
+    async def test_open_binary_write(self, tmp_path):
+        """Test opening and writing to a binary file."""
+        fpath = str(tmp_path / "write_test.bin")
+        async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "wb") as f:
+            n = await f.write(b"\xde\xad\xbe\xef")
+        assert n == 4
+        with open(fpath, "rb") as f:
+            assert f.read() == b"\xde\xad\xbe\xef"
+
+    async def test_open_append(self, tmp_path):
+        """Test append mode."""
+        fpath = str(tmp_path / "append_test.txt")
+        with open(fpath, "w") as f:
+            f.write("First line\n")
+        async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "a") as f:
+            await f.write("Second line\n")
+        with open(fpath, "r") as f:
+            assert f.read() == "First line\nSecond line\n"
+
+    async def test_open_readplus(self, tmp_path):
+        """Test read+write mode (r+)."""
+        fpath = str(tmp_path / "rplus_test.txt")
+        with open(fpath, "w") as f:
+            f.write("Original")
+        async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "r+") as f:
+            content = await f.read()
+            assert content == "Original"
+            await f.write(" Appended")
+        with open(fpath, "r") as f:
+            assert f.read() == "Original Appended"
+
+    async def test_open_wbplus(self, tmp_path):
+        """Test wb+ mode."""
+        fpath = str(tmp_path / "wbplus_test.bin")
+        async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "wb+") as f:
+            await f.write(b"binary data")
+            await f.seek(0)
+            data = await f.read()
+        assert data == b"binary data"
+
+    async def test_open_encoding(self, tmp_path):
+        """Test opening with specific encoding."""
+        fpath = str(tmp_path / "utf8_test.txt")
+        async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
+            await f.write("Hello 世界")
+        async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
+            content = await f.read()
+        assert content == "Hello 世界"
+
+    async def test_open_nonexistent_raises(self):
+        """Test that opening a nonexistent file raises FileNotFoundError."""
+        with pytest.raises(FileNotFoundError):
+            async with aiofiles.open("/nonexistent/path/file.txt", "r"):
+                pass
+
+    async def test_open_buffering_zero(self, sample_binary_file):
+        """Test opening with buffering=0 (raw FileIO)."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_binary_file, "rb", buffering=0) as f:
+            # With buffering=0, we get AsyncFileIO
+            data = await f.read()
+        assert data == b"\x00\x01\x02\x03\x04\x05"
+
+    async def test_open_returns_context_manager(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test that aiofiles.open returns a context manager."""
+        cm = aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r")
+        # Should be an AiofilesContextManager
+        assert hasattr(cm, "__aenter__")
+        assert hasattr(cm, "__aexit__")
+        # Also has __await__
+        assert hasattr(cm, "__await__")
+        # Clean up: actually open and close
+        async with cm as f:
+            await f.read()
+
+    async def test_open_file_closed_on_exit(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test that file is properly closed on context manager exit."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+            await f.read()
+            assert not f.closed
+        assert f.closed
+
+    async def test_open_close_on_exception(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test that file is closed even when an exception occurs."""
+        try:
+            async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+                raise ValueError("test exception")
+        except ValueError:
+            pass
+        assert f.closed

+"""Tests for async standard I/O stream operations."""
+
+import sys
+import io
+import pytest
+import aiofiles
+from aiofiles.threadpool import stdin, stdout, stderr
+from aiofiles.threadpool import stdin_bytes, stdout_bytes, stderr_bytes
+
+
+class TestStdout:
+    """Test async stdout operations."""
+
+    async def test_stdout_write(self, capsys):
+        """Test writing to async stdout."""
+        await aiofiles.stdout.write("hello stdout\n")
+        captured = capsys.readouterr()
+        assert "hello stdout" in captured.out
+
+    async def test_stdout_write_multiple(self, capsys):
+        """Test multiple writes to stdout."""
+        await aiofiles.stdout.write("first\n")
+        await aiofiles.stdout.write("second\n")
+        captured = capsys.readouterr()
+        assert "first" in captured.out
+        assert "second" in captured.out
+
+    async def test_stdout_flush(self, capsys):
+        """Test flushing stdout."""
+        await aiofiles.stdout.write("flush test\n")
+        await aiofiles.stdout.flush()
+        captured = capsys.readouterr()
+        assert "flush test" in captured.out
+
+    async def test_stdout_writable(self):
+        """Test stdout is writable."""
+        assert await aiofiles.stdout.writable()
+
+    async def test_stdout_readable(self):
+        """Test stdout readable() returns a boolean."""
+        result = await aiofiles.stdout.readable()
+        assert isinstance(result, bool)
+
+    async def test_stdout_name(self):
+        """Test stdout name property."""
+        name = stdout.name
+        assert name is not None
+
+    async def test_stdout_close_property(self):
+        """Test stdout closed property."""
+        closed = stdout.closed
+
+
+class TestStderr:
+    """Test async stderr operations."""
+
+    async def test_stderr_write(self, capsys):
+        """Test writing to async stderr."""
+        await aiofiles.stderr.write("error message\n")
+        captured = capsys.readouterr()
+        assert "error message" in captured.err
+
+    async def test_stderr_write_multiple(self, capsys):
+        """Test multiple writes to stderr."""
+        await aiofiles.stderr.write("err1\n")
+        await aiofiles.stderr.write("err2\n")
+        captured = capsys.readouterr()
+        assert "err1" in captured.err
+        assert "err2" in captured.err
+
+    async def test_stderr_flush(self, capsys):
+        """Test flushing stderr."""
+        await aiofiles.stderr.write("flush err\n")
+        await aiofiles.stderr.flush()
+        captured = capsys.readouterr()
+        assert "flush err" in captured.err
+
+    async def test_stderr_writable(self):
+        """Test stderr is writable."""
+        assert await aiofiles.stderr.writable()
+
+    async def test_stderr_readable(self):
+        """Test stderr readable() returns a boolean."""

+        assert isinstance(result, bool)
+
+
+class TestStdin:

+    async def test_stdout_bytes_flush(self, capsys):
+        """Test flushing stdout_bytes."""

+        await aiofiles.stdout_bytes.flush()

+
+
+class TestStderrBytes:
+    """Test async stderr_bytes operations."""
+
+    async def test_stderr_bytes_write(self, capsys):
+        """Test writing bytes to stderr_bytes."""
+        await aiofiles.stderr_bytes.write(b"binary error\n")
+        captured = capsys.readouterr()
+        assert "binary error" in captured.err
+
+    async def test_stderr_bytes_writable(self):
+        """Test stderr_bytes is writable."""
+        assert await aiofiles.stderr_bytes.writable()
+
+    async def test_stderr_bytes_readable(self):
+        """Test stderr_bytes readable() returns a boolean (sync method)."""
+        result = aiofiles.stderr_bytes.readable()
+        assert isinstance(result, bool)
+
+
+class TestStdinBytes:
+    """Test async stdin_bytes operations."""
+
+    async def test_stdin_bytes_readable(self):
+        """Test stdin_bytes readable() returns a boolean (sync method)."""
+        result = aiofiles.stdin_bytes.readable()
+        assert isinstance(result, bool)
+
+    async def test_stdin_bytes_writable(self):
+        """Test stdin_bytes is not writable."""
+        assert not await aiofiles.stdin_bytes.writable()
+
+
+class TestStdioImports:
+    """Test that stdio objects are properly exported."""
+
+    async def test_import_from_aiofiles(self):
+        """Test importing stdio from aiofiles."""
+        from aiofiles import stdin, stdout, stderr
+        from aiofiles import stdin_bytes, stdout_bytes, stderr_bytes
+        assert stdin is aiofiles.stdin
+        assert stdout is aiofiles.stdout
+        assert stderr is aiofiles.stderr
+        assert stdin_bytes is aiofiles.stdin_bytes
+        assert stdout_bytes is aiofiles.stdout_bytes
+        assert stderr_bytes is aiofiles.stderr_bytes
+
+    async def test_import_from_threadpool(self):
+        """Test importing stdio from aiofiles.threadpool."""
+        from aiofiles.threadpool import stdin as tp_stdin
+        from aiofiles.threadpool import stdout as tp_stdout
+        from aiofiles.threadpool import stderr as tp_stderr
+        from aiofiles.threadpool import stdin_bytes as tp_stdin_bytes
+        from aiofiles.threadpool import stdout_bytes as tp_stdout_bytes
+        from aiofiles.threadpool import stderr_bytes as tp_stderr_bytes
+        assert tp_stdin is aiofiles.stdin
+        assert tp_stdout is aiofiles.stdout
+        assert tp_stderr is aiofiles.stderr

+"""Tests for async text file operations."""
+
+import asyncio
+import pytest
+import aiofiles
+
+
+class TestTextRead:
+    """Test text file reading operations."""
+
+    async def test_read_all(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test read() reads entire text file."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+            content = await f.read()
+        assert content == "Line 1\nLine 2\nLine 3\n"
+        assert isinstance(content, str)
+
+    async def test_read_partial(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test read(n) reads up to n characters."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+            content = await f.read(6)
+        assert content == "Line 1"
+
+    async def test_read_zero(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test read(0) returns empty string."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+            content = await f.read(0)
+        assert content == ""
+
+    async def test_read_after_eof(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test reading past EOF returns empty string."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+            data1 = await f.read()
+            data2 = await f.read()
+        assert data2 == ""
+
+    async def test_readline(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test readline() returns one line at a time."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+            line1 = await f.readline()
+            line2 = await f.readline()
+        assert line1 == "Line 1\n"
+        assert line2 == "Line 2\n"
+
+    async def test_readline_eof(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test readline() at EOF returns empty string."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+            await f.read()  # consume all
+            line = await f.readline()
+        assert line == ""
+
+    async def test_readlines(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test readlines() returns all lines."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+            lines = await f.readlines()
+        assert lines == ["Line 1\n", "Line 2\n", "Line 3\n"]
+
+    async def test_readlines_empty_file(self, tmp_path):
+        """Test readlines() on empty file."""
+        fpath = str(tmp_path / "empty.txt")
+        with open(fpath, "w"):
+            pass
+        async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "r") as f:
+            lines = await f.readlines()
+        assert lines == []
+
+    async def test_read_large_file(self, sample_large_file):
+        """Test reading a larger file."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_large_file, "r") as f:
+            content = await f.read()
+        lines = content.strip().split("\n")
+        assert len(lines) == 100
+        assert lines[0] == "Line 0"
+        assert lines[-1] == "Line 99"
+
+
+class TestTextWrite:
+    """Test text file writing operations."""
+
+    async def test_write(self, tmp_path):
+        """Test write() on text file."""
+        fpath = str(tmp_path / "write.txt")
+        async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "w") as f:
+            n = await f.write("Hello aiofiles")
+        assert n == 14
+        with open(fpath, "r") as f:
+            assert f.read() == "Hello aiofiles"
+
+    async def test_write_multiline(self, tmp_path):
+        """Test writing multiple lines."""
+        fpath = str(tmp_path / "multiline.txt")
+        async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "w") as f:
+            await f.write("Line 1\nLine 2\nLine 3\n")
+        with open(fpath, "r") as f:
+            assert f.read() == "Line 1\nLine 2\nLine 3\n"
+
+    async def test_writelines(self, tmp_path):
+        """Test writelines() on text file."""
+        fpath = str(tmp_path / "writelines.txt")
+        lines = ["line1\n", "line2\n", "line3\n"]
+        async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "w") as f:
+            await f.writelines(lines)
+        with open(fpath, "r") as f:
+            assert f.read() == "line1\nline2\nline3\n"
+
+    async def test_writelines_no_newlines(self, tmp_path):
+        """Test writelines() does not add newlines."""
+        fpath = str(tmp_path / "writelines2.txt")
+        lines = ["a", "b", "c"]
+        async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "w") as f:
+            await f.writelines(lines)
+        with open(fpath, "r") as f:
+            assert f.read() == "abc"
+
+    async def test_flush(self, tmp_path):
+        """Test flush() on text file."""
+        fpath = str(tmp_path / "flush.txt")
+        async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "w") as f:
+            await f.write("data")
+            await f.flush()
+        with open(fpath, "r") as f:
+            assert f.read() == "data"
+
+    async def test_truncate(self, tmp_path):
+        """Test truncate() on text file."""
+        fpath = str(tmp_path / "truncate.txt")
+        async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "w+") as f:
+            await f.write("Hello World")
+            await f.seek(5)
+            await f.truncate()
+        with open(fpath, "r") as f:
+            assert f.read() == "Hello"
+
+
+class TestTextPositioning:
+    """Test text file positioning and attribute operations."""
+
+    async def test_tell(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test tell() returns current position."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+            assert await f.tell() == 0
+            await f.read(6)
+            pos = await f.tell()
+            assert pos > 0
+
+    async def test_seek(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test seek() on text file."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+            await f.seek(7)
+            data = await f.read(6)
+        assert data == "Line 2"
+
+    async def test_seekable(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test seekable() returns True."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+            assert await f.seekable()
+
+    async def test_readable(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test readable() returns True."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+            assert await f.readable()
+
+    async def test_writable(self, tmp_path):
+        """Test writable() returns True for write mode."""
+        fpath = str(tmp_path / "w.txt")
+        async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "w") as f:
+            assert await f.writable()
+
+    async def test_isatty(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test isatty() returns False."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+            assert not await f.isatty()
+
+    async def test_fileno(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test fileno() returns integer."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+            fd = f.fileno()
+        assert isinstance(fd, int)
+        assert fd >= 0
+
+
+class TestTextProperties:
+    """Test text file property access."""
+
+    async def test_closed_property(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test closed property."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+            assert not f.closed
+        assert f.closed
+
+    async def test_name_property(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test name property."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+            assert f.name == sample_text_file
+
+    async def test_mode_property(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test mode property."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+            assert f.mode == "r"
+
+    async def test_encoding_property(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test encoding property."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
+            assert f.encoding == "utf-8"
+
+    async def test_buffer_property(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test buffer property exists."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+            assert f.buffer is not None
+
+    async def test_errors_property(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test errors property."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r", errors="strict") as f:
+            assert f.errors == "strict"
+
+    async def test_newlines_property(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test newlines property."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+            await f.read()
+            # After reading, newlines should be set
+            nl = f.newlines
+
+    async def test_line_buffering_property(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test line_buffering property."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+            lb = f.line_buffering
+        assert isinstance(lb, bool)
+
+
+class TestTextIteration:
+    """Test async iteration over text files."""
+
+    async def test_async_for_iteration(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test async for iteration reads lines."""
+        lines = []
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+            async for line in f:
+                lines.append(line)
+        assert lines == ["Line 1\n", "Line 2\n", "Line 3\n"]
+
+    async def test_async_for_empty_file(self, tmp_path):
+        """Test async for on empty file."""
+        fpath = str(tmp_path / "empty.txt")
+        with open(fpath, "w"):
+            pass
+        lines = []
+        async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "r") as f:
+            async for line in f:
+                lines.append(line)
+        assert lines == []
+
+    async def test_async_for_large_file(self, sample_large_file):
+        """Test async for on larger file."""
+        lines = []
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_large_file, "r") as f:
+            async for line in f:
+                lines.append(line)
+        assert len(lines) == 100
+
+    async def test_aiter_returns_self(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test __aiter__ returns self."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+            assert f.__aiter__() is f
+
+    async def test_anext_raises(self, tmp_path):
+        """Test __anext__ raises StopAsyncIteration at EOF."""
+        fpath = str(tmp_path / "empty.txt")
+        with open(fpath, "w"):
+            pass
+        async with aiofiles.open(fpath, "r") as f:
+            with pytest.raises(StopAsyncIteration):
+                await f.__anext__()
+
+    async def test_anext_returns_line(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test __anext__ returns next line."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+            line = await f.__anext__()
+        assert line == "Line 1\n"
+
+    async def test_repr(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test __repr__ includes wrapped file repr."""
+        async with aiofiles.open(sample_text_file, "r") as f:
+            r = repr(f)
+        assert "wrapping" in r

+++ b/tests/threadpool/test_wrap.py
@@ -0,0 +1,145 @@
+import sys
+"""Tests for the wrap() function (singledispatch file wrapper)."""
+
+import io
+import pytest
+import asyncio
+from aiofiles.threadpool import wrap
+
+
+class TestWrapTextIOWrapper:
+    """Test wrapping TextIOWrapper objects."""
+
+    async def test_wrap_textiowrapper(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test wrapping a text file object."""
+        with open(sample_text_file, "r") as sync_file:
+            async_file = wrap(sync_file)
+            content = await async_file.read()
+        assert content == "Line 1\nLine 2\nLine 3\n"
+
+    async def test_wrap_stringio(self):
+        """Test wrapping a StringIO object."""
+        text_file = io.StringIO("Hello World")
+        async_file = wrap(text_file)
+        content = await async_file.read()
+        assert content == "Hello World"
+
+    async def test_wrap_text_write(self):
+        """Test writing through wrapped text file."""
+        text_file = io.StringIO()
+        async_file = wrap(text_file)
+        n = await async_file.write("test data")
+        assert n == 9
+        text_file.seek(0)
+        assert text_file.read() == "test data"
+
+    async def test_wrap_text_seek_tell(self):
+        """Test seek/tell on wrapped text file."""
+        text_file = io.StringIO("Hello World")
+        async_file = wrap(text_file)
+        assert await async_file.tell() == 0
+        await async_file.seek(6)
+        assert await async_file.tell() == 6
+        data = await async_file.read()
+        assert data == "World"
+
+
+class TestWrapBinaryIO:
+    """Test wrapping binary file objects."""
+
+    async def test_wrap_bytesio(self):
+        """Test wrapping a BytesIO object."""
+        binary_file = io.BytesIO(b"Binary data here")
+        async_file = wrap(binary_file)
+        data = await async_file.read()
+        assert data == b"Binary data here"
+
+    async def test_wrap_bytesio_write(self):
+        """Test writing through wrapped binary file."""
+        binary_file = io.BytesIO()
+        async_file = wrap(binary_file)
+        n = await async_file.write(b"test")
+        assert n == 4
+        binary_file.seek(0)
+        assert binary_file.read() == b"test"
+
+    async def test_wrap_fileio(self, sample_binary_file):
+        """Test wrapping a FileIO object."""
+        with io.FileIO(sample_binary_file, "rb") as raw:
+            async_file = wrap(raw)
+            data = await async_file.readall()
+        assert data == b"\x00\x01\x02\x03\x04\x05"
+
+    async def test_wrap_bufferedreader(self, sample_binary_file):
+        """Test wrapping a BufferedReader."""
+        with open(sample_binary_file, "rb") as raw:
+            buffered = io.BufferedReader(raw)
+            async_file = wrap(buffered)
+            data = await async_file.read()
+        assert data == b"\x00\x01\x02\x03\x04\x05"
+
+    async def test_wrap_bufferedwriter(self, tmp_path):
+        """Test wrapping a BufferedWriter."""
+        fpath = str(tmp_path / "bufwrite.bin")
+        raw = io.open(fpath, "wb", buffering=0)
+        buffered = io.BufferedWriter(raw)
+        async_file = wrap(buffered)
+        await async_file.write(b"hello")
+        await async_file.flush()
+        buffered.detach()
+        with open(fpath, "rb") as f:
+            assert f.read() == b"hello"
+
+    async def test_wrap_unsupported_raises(self):
+        """Test wrapping unsupported type raises TypeError."""
+        with pytest.raises(TypeError, match="Unsupported IO type"):
+            wrap(42)
+
+
+class TestWrapStdio:
+    """Test wrapping standard I/O streams."""
+
+    async def test_wrap_stdout(self):
+        """Test wrapping stdout."""
+        saved = sys.stdout
+        sys.stdout = io.StringIO()
+        try:
+            async_file = wrap(sys.stdout)
+            await async_file.write("test output")
+            sys.stdout.seek(0)
+            assert sys.stdout.read() == "test output"
+        finally:
+            sys.stdout = saved
+
+    async def test_wrap_stdin(self):
+        """Test wrapping stdin."""
+        saved = sys.stdin
+        sys.stdin = io.StringIO("input data\n")
+        try:
+            async_file = wrap(sys.stdin)
+            data = await async_file.read()
+            assert data == "input data\n"
+        finally:
+            sys.stdin = saved
+
+
+class TestWrapProperties:
+    """Test properties on wrapped objects."""
+
+    async def test_wrapped_name(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test name property on wrapped file."""
+        with open(sample_text_file, "r") as sync_file:
+            async_file = wrap(sync_file)
+            assert async_file.name == sample_text_file
+
+    async def test_wrapped_mode(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test mode property on wrapped file."""
+        with open(sample_text_file, "r") as sync_file:
+            async_file = wrap(sync_file)
+            assert async_file.mode == "r"
+
+    async def test_wrapped_closed(self, sample_text_file):
+        """Test closed property on wrapped file."""
+        with open(sample_text_file, "r") as sync_file:
+            async_file = wrap(sync_file)
+            assert not async_file.closed
